body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule P 2025 tax credits respecting the work premium.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.P-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule P numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 200
Value: 19534
Value: 26946
Value: 12620
Value: 17798
Value: 3600
Value: 2400
Value: 1200
Value: 25
Value: 0.25
Value: 11.6
Value: 0.116
Value: 20
Value: 0.20
Value: 13.6
Value: 0.136
Value: 30
Value: 0.30
Value: 10
Value: 0.10

Schedule P Part A calculates work income. For the taxpayer, line 18 adds lines 10 through 15: employment income from line 101 plus positive line 105 minus RL-1 box 211 if applicable, line 107 income, net research grants, total Schedule L lines 22 through 26.1 excluding losses, and WEPP payments. Line 29 subtracts line 22 from line 18. For the spouse, line 38 adds lines 30 through 35 and line 49 subtracts line 42 from line 38.

Part C calculates family income. Line 54 adds the taxpayer's line 275 net income on line 52 and the spouse's line 275 net income on line 53.

Part D calculates the supplement to the work premium. Line 56 is $200. Line 59 uses the lesser of total months in RL-5 box V on line 57 and eligible months in 2025 on line 58. Line 60 multiplies line 56 by line 59 and carries the result to line 88.

Part E calculates the work premium and adapted work premium. With a spouse on December 31, 2025, line 64 adds lines 29 and 49. The work premium column uses a line 66 cap and line 80 family-income threshold of $19,534 and a line 70 base of $3,600. The adapted work premium column uses a line 66 cap and line 80 family-income threshold of $26,946 and a line 70 base of $1,200. Without a spouse, line 64 uses line 29. The work premium column uses a line 66 cap and line 80 family-income threshold of $12,620 and a line 70 base of $2,400. The adapted work premium column uses a line 66 cap and line 80 family-income threshold of $17,798 and a line 70 base of $1,200.

For the work premium column, line 74 is 25% with a designated child and 11.6% without a designated child for individuals with a spouse, and 30% with a designated child and 11.6% without a designated child for individuals without a spouse. For the adapted work premium column, line 75 is 20% with a designated child and 13.6% without a designated child for individuals with a spouse, and 25% with a designated child and 13.6% without a designated child for individuals without a spouse. Line 68 uses the lesser of line 64 and line 66. Line 72 subtracts line 70 from line 68 and floors the result at zero. Line 76 multiplies line 72 by line 74 or line 75. Line 82 subtracts line 80 from line 78 and floors the result at zero. Line 83 multiplies line 82 by 10%. Line 84 subtracts line 83 from line 76 and floors the result at zero. Line 85 uses the greater of the line 84 amounts in column 1 and column 2. With a spouse, line 87 subtracts the spouse's line 89 of Schedule P from line 85 and carries the result to line 89. Without a spouse, line 87 carries the greater column amount to line 89. Part F line 90 adds line 88 and line 89 and carries the tax credits respecting the work premium to TP-1 line 456.
